metrics.narrative: Vestacy articulated operational pain but did not quantify financial impact. On the 2026-06-05 discovery call, Marie Comminges (Jr Buyer) stated they are consolidating procurement post-carve-out from Reckitt, managing 1 billion EUR annual spend, and operating under a Transition Service Agreement (TSA) with Reckitt that is ending 'soon' (no specific date given). The business problem is clear: they need an independent Source-to-Contract platform before TSA expiration to avoid operational disruption and achieve procurement autonomy. Pain points include Dynamics 365 native P2P limitations (no strategic sourcing, contract management, supplier risk, or AI-driven intake), manual processes, and lack of visibility into supplier performance and contract compliance. However, no quantified metrics were provided (e.g., cost of TSA extension, cycle-time reduction target, savings goal, headcount efficiency). The next move is to surface and quantify the cost of inaction (TSA extension cost, manual process burden, compliance risk exposure) during the post-NDA requirements walkthrough session, and to build a business case anchoring ROI to Vestacy's 1B EUR spend base.

They have not introduced Zycus to the Global Procurement Director, CPO Alessandro Roveda, or any other senior stakeholder. They have not provided competitive intelligence (who else is in the RFP, what other vendors are being evaluated, internal preferences or biases). They have not shared internal documents (draft RFP, business case, architecture diagrams) despite promising to do so post-NDA. Engagement is active (last contact 9 days ago per LastActivityDate 2026-06-24) but transactional, not strategic. The next move is to assess whether Marie or Ana Sofia can and will advocate for Zycus internally, and to request they facilitate introductions to the Global Procurement Director and CPO during the post-NDA requirements session, positioning Zycus as a strategic partner (not just an RFP respondent) and building executive-level sponsorship to win this deal.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is confirmed as a competitor; other vendors likely but not named. On the 2026-06-05 discovery call, Marie Comminges stated 'we are evaluating SAP Ariba as well' when discussing the RFP process. No other competitors were named on the call or in Salesforce fields (Competitors__c null or not present). The do-nothing scenario (continuing with Dynamics 365 native P2P only, potentially extending the TSA with Reckitt) is a de facto competitor, though the TSA expiration forcing function makes this unlikely. Marie's comment 'we are including you in the RFP process' signals a multi-vendor evaluation, so expect 2 to 3 additional vendors (likely Coupa, GEP, Ivalua based on typical enterprise S2C RFP shortlists, but not confirmed). The competitive wedge against Ariba is Merlin AI differentiation (Ariba has no agentic AI intake or negotiation capability), Dynamics 365 prebuilt integration (Ariba-to-Dynamics integration is more complex and often requires middleware), user adoption and ease of use (Zycus modern UX vs Ariba legacy interface), and implementation speed (Zycus in-house team vs Ariba consultancy-dependent model, critical given TSA timeline pressure). The next move is to surface competitive intelligence during the requirements walkthrough session (ask Marie who else is in the RFP, what Vestacy likes/dislikes about each vendor), and to position Zycus differentiation aggressively in the RFP response: lead with Merlin AI (tie to their AI appetite if validated), emphasize Dynamics 365 integration depth (deliver the Rishi deep-dive to prove it), and anchor implementation speed to the TSA forcing function.

identify_pain.narrative: Vestacy's pain is clear and tied to a forcing event. They were carved out from Reckitt and are operating under a Transition Service Agreement (TSA) for procurement services that is ending 'soon' (specific date not disclosed). On the 2026-06-05 discovery call, Marie Comminges stated 'we are under a TSA with Reckitt and it is ending soon, so we need our own procurement platform in place before that happens.' The operational pain is procurement platform dependency and lack of autonomy: they cannot run independent strategic sourcing, contract management, supplier risk management, or AI-driven intake on Dynamics 365 native P2P alone (Ana Sofia Iniesta: 'Dynamics 365 does not cover strategic sourcing, contract management, supplier risk, or AI intake, we need a full Source-to-Contract suite'). The financial pain is the cost and risk of TSA extension (not quantified) and the inefficiency of manual procurement processes across 1 billion EUR annual spend. The pain owner is the Procurement Excellence & Digitalization team (Marie, Ana Sofia) reporting to the Global Procurement Director, with ultimate accountability to CPO Alessandro Roveda. The urgency is real (TSA expiration forcing function) but not quantified with a specific deadline, which creates timeline risk. The pain is confirmed across multiple sources and drives the RFP process, but the cost of inaction has not been quantified.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process and contracting mechanics are unknown. No discussion of MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, or implementation scoping has occurred. The NDA requirement is the only contracting touchpoint so far: Vestacy requires Zycus to sign their NDA before sharing business case, architecture, and expectations (Next Step June update, discovery call 2026-06-05). No Salesforce fields (Mandate__c, Current_Contract_Expiration__c) contain contracting or paper-process information. For an enterprise procurement platform purchase, expect a multi-stage contracting process: NDA (in progress), RFP response and demo phase, vendor selection, commercial negotiation, MSA and SOW drafting, legal and procurement review, InfoSec and data privacy review (especially for supplier data and ERP integration), implementation scoping (Dynamics 365 integration, data migration, user training), and executive sign-off. The TSA expiration date is a forcing function but has not been quantified. The next move is to map the end-to-end timeline from RFP to go-live during the requirements walkthrough session, explicitly asking for the TSA end date and working backward to validate feasibility, and to surface Zycus implementation speed advantage (in-house team, faster than consultancy-dependent competitors) to de-risk timeline pressure.

decision_criteria.narrative: Vestacy has articulated high-level evaluation priorities but has not yet released the formal decision criteria, which will come with the RFP in late June 2026. On the 2026-06-05 discovery call, Ana Sofia Iniesta and Marie Comminges outlined their top requirements: full Source-to-Contract suite (strategic sourcing, contract management buy-side and sell-side, supplier onboarding and risk, eProcurement, AI-driven intake via Merlin), Dynamics 365 integration (seamless P2P handoff, supplier master sync, approval workflow integration, ERP data model alignment), user adoption and ease of use (they want a modern, intuitive platform their users will actually use), and speed to value (they are under time pressure due to TSA expiration). Marie explicitly stated 'integration with Dynamics 365 is one of our top 3 decision criteria' and requested a dedicated integration deep-dive session with Zycus specialist Rishi to validate prebuilt connectors, data flow, and architecture. The formal RFP will contain detailed requirements, weightings, and scoring criteria. The next move is to shape the RFP by surfacing Zycus strengths (Merlin AI differentiation, Dynamics 365 prebuilt connectors, in-house implementation speed advantage, user adoption methodology) during the requirements walkthrough session and in the RFP response, and to deliver the integration deep-dive before RFP release to de-risk integration complexity as a decision factor.
